matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: SHANK3 neurobiology variation model (SHANK3 neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model)
SHANK3 regulatory expression context model (SHANK3 expression and methylation-context model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SHANK3 neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: SHANK3 neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed SHANK3 neurobiology variation model dosage suggests this sample carries the SHANK3 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a SHANK3 neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. This specific marker is curated as SHANK3 neurological and neurodegenerative biology pathway model. SHANK3 neurobiology variation model is curated as a SHANK3 marker for Neurological, neurodevelopmental, and neurodegenerative disease context, Synaptic signaling, excitability, neurotransmitter, and protein-homeostasis biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched SHANK3 neurobiology variation model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the SHANK3 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SHANK3 neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a SHANK3 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SHANK3 neurological and neurodegenerative biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. SHANK3 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local SHANK3 bundle is oriented toward neurological and neurodegenerative biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation. SHANK3 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize synaptic signaling, neurotransmission, protein aggregation, neuronal survival, neurodevelopment, and neurodegeneration biology.
